name: Allmand Law
description_short: Texas-based bankruptcy law firm led by Board-Certified attorney Reed Allmand, specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings across 17 cities in Texas.
description_long:
Allmand Law is a consumer bankruptcy firm founded and led by Reed Allmand, a Board-Certified Bankruptcy Attorney certified by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization. The firm operates across Texas with service areas spanning from Dallas-Fort Worth to Houston, San Antonio, Austin, and Waco. Allmand's background stems from a personal history of financial struggle in childhood, which motivated him to pursue law as a means to serve families in financial distress.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, foreclosure defense, credit repair consultation, medical debt resolution, student loan debt guidance, tax forgiveness and resolution, stopping creditor harassment, preventing vehicle repossession, and stopping evictions. They provide free case evaluations and free financial empowerment sessions to prospective clients. The firm also maintains a client portal for ongoing case management and offers multilingual support (English and Spanish).

Allmand Law differentiates itself through several factors: Reed Allmand's Board Certification in Consumer Bankruptcy (a credential held by relatively few attorneys), claims to be the largest consumer bankruptcy firm in Texas, has handled thousands of cases over 20+ years, maintains an educational content library (blog, videos, FAQs), and emphasizes a compassion-first approach with clear communication. The firm has received media recognition from Fox and CBS.

However, prospective clients should note that Allmand Law is exclusively a bankruptcy law firm—they do not provide general debt settlement, debt management plans, or credit repair in the traditional sense. While they address credit repair topics, their core service is filing bankruptcy petitions. The firm's reputation and track record appear solid, but bankruptcy should only be considered after consultation, as it has long-term credit consequences.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Board-Certified in Consumer Bankruptcy by Texas Board of Legal Specialization (Reed Allmand)
Claims to be the largest consumer bankruptcy firm in Texas with 20+ years and thousands of cases handled
Free case evaluation and free financial empowerment session offered to all prospective clients
Serves 17+ cities across Texas including major metros (Dallas, Houston, Austin, San Antonio, Fort Worth)
Comprehensive service offerings beyond filing (foreclosure defense, credit repair guidance, tax resolution, student loan debt, medical debt)
Multilingual support (English and Spanish) with client portal for ongoing case management
Featured on major media outlets (Fox & CBS) indicating credibility and recognition
cons: Bankruptcy has severe long-term credit consequences (7-10 year reporting period) that are not heavily emphasized on the site
No pricing information disclosed—typical bankruptcy filing costs are not transparent on the website
Limited information about alternative debt relief options (debt consolidation, management plans) that may be more appropriate for some situations
Firm exclusively handles bankruptcy; does not offer debt settlement or debt management services for those who do not qualify for or want to avoid bankruptcy
No specific information about success rates, client outcomes, or case statistics to substantiate 'largest firm' claim
best_for: Texas residents with significant unsecured debt (credit cards, medical bills) seeking Chapter 7 liquidation
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need immediate legal intervention and want to explore bankruptcy options
Individuals with stable income considering Chapter 13 reorganization plans to repay debt over 3-5 years
People experiencing creditor harassment or wage garnishment seeking automatic stay protection
diagnosis: Allmand Law is best for Texas residents with substantial debt who have determined bankruptcy is appropriate and need professional legal representation for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filings. The primary caveat is that bankruptcy should only be pursued after careful consideration with counsel, as it severely impacts credit for 7-10 years; clients should ensure they've explored all alternatives before filing.
services: Means test analysis and qualification assessment
Foreclosure defense and stopping foreclosure proceedings
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 85 NE Interstate 410 Loop Ste.217, San Antonio, TX 78216
